meaning: n.[医] 利什曼病(尤指黑热病)
examples: If left untreated, visceral leishmaniasis is usually fatal.
Leishmaniasis is transmitted by sandflies and possibly through sharing needles.
He said two of them are suffering from a skin disease known as leishmaniasis, which they contracted during captivity.
OneWorld Health is currently developing medicines for diarrheal diseases, malaria, visceral leishmaniasis and soil-transmitted helminthes (intestinal worms).
In addition the goal is to reduce and control these diseases: schistosomiasis, river blindness, soil-transmitted helminthes, Chagas disease and visceral leishmaniasis.
